one_line_positioning: NIFT-trained fashion merchandiser who builds categories from zero, driving 15%+ sales growth and margin improvement
narrative_angle: Entrepreneurial merchandiser with a track record of launching and scaling fashion categories across online and offline channels, using data-driven assortment planning and vendor negotiation to maximize profitability.
lead_strengths: Assortment Planning & Category Expansion (expanded from 5 to 25+ subcategories)
Vendor Management & Negotiation (onboarded 12+ vendors, margin improvement from 22% to 35%)
Womenswear Trend-Driven Assortment (managed bottom-wear at Go Colors, launched western-wear brand)
P&L Ownership & Inventory Optimization (reduced stock-outs by 20%, maintained 45% gross margins)
proof_points_to_emphasize: Built fashion category from near zero, generating ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months at SuperK
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% while maintaining competitive pricing through vendor negotiations
Launched 3+ new sub-categories at Go Colors, boosting category sales by 15% in the first quarter
Reduced stock-outs by ~20% across priority products at SuperK through inventory flow coordination
Zero returns on 80+ orders for own brand The Style Gram, demonstrating quality and fit accuracy
things_to_downplay: Limited experience in large-scale fast-fashion environments
tone_guidance: Action-oriented, metric-driven, and entrepreneurial. Emphasize builder mindset and rapid results.
recruiter_first_impression_target: A hands-on fashion merchandiser who thinks like a business owner and can turn data into profitable assortments
reasoning: Strong alignment with core merchandising must-haves (assortment planning, vendor management, womenswear trend, Advanced Excel, omnichannel) and nice-to-haves (NIFT, category launch, BI tools, WIP trackers). Missing critical OTB management and SAP proficiency, but P&L ownership and inventory optimization suggest transferable financial planning skills. Total 4.1 years meets minimum, but candidate's rapid category-building achievements offset experience length. Risk: no direct fast-fashion experience; value retail and D2C background may not fully align with H&M's global fast-fashion model. CAREFUL_PUSH due to OTB gap; highlight ability to learn quickly and P&L acumen.
